models.name: Bootstrapping the Zero Curve
models.short_description: Sequentially extract zero-coupon rates from observed coupon bond prices to build the foundational discount curve used across all fixed income pricing.
models.key_insight: Each successive bond maturity reveals one new zero rate, building the curve sequentially from short to long maturities.

models.name: Nelson-Siegel Model
models.short_description: A parsimonious parametric model that captures the level, slope, and curvature of the yield curve with just four parameters, widely used by central banks.
models.key_insight: Three factors (level, slope, curvature) explain >95% of yield curve variation.
